Submodules

news.admin module

class news.admin.ApprovedArticleLogAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin configuration for approved article logs.

list_display = ['article', 'posted_at']
list_filter = ['posted_at']
property media
readonly_fields = ['posted_at']
search_fields = ['article__title']
class news.admin.ArticleAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin configuration for articles.

list_display = ['title', 'author', 'publisher', 'approved', 'created_at']
list_filter = ['approved', 'publisher', 'created_at']
property media
readonly_fields = ['created_at']
search_fields = ['title', 'content', 'author__username', 'publisher__name']
class news.admin.CustomUserAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]

Bases: UserAdmin

Admin configuration for the custom user model.

Adds the role and subscription fields to the Django admin.

add_fieldsets = ((None, {'classes': ('wide',), 'fields': ('username', 'password1', 'password2')}), ('News Application Role Information', {'fields': ('email', 'role')}))
fieldsets = ((None, {'fields': ('username', 'password')}), ('Personal info', {'fields': ('first_name', 'last_name', 'email')}), ('Permissions', {'fields': ('is_active', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser', 'groups', 'user_permissions')}), ('Important dates', {'fields': ('last_login', 'date_joined')}), ('News Application Role Information', {'fields': ('role', 'subscribed_publishers', 'subscribed_journalists')}))
filter_horizontal = ['groups', 'user_permissions', 'subscribed_publishers', 'subscribed_journalists']
list_display = ['username', 'email', 'role', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser']
list_filter = ['role', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser', 'is_active']
property media
search_fields = ['username', 'email']
class news.admin.NewsletterAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin configuration for newsletters.

filter_horizontal = ['articles']
list_display = ['title', 'author', 'created_at']
list_filter = ['created_at', 'author']
property media
readonly_fields = ['created_at']
search_fields = ['title', 'description', 'author__username']
class news.admin.PublisherAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin configuration for publishers.

filter_horizontal = ['editors', 'journalists']
list_display = ['name', 'description']
property media
search_fields = ['name']

class news.apps.NewsConfig(app_name, app_module)[source]

Bases: AppConfig

default_auto_field = 'django.db.models.BigAutoField'
name = 'news'
ready()[source]

Override this method in subclasses to run code when Django starts.

class news.forms.RegisterForm(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: UserCreationForm

Form used to register new users with a selected role.

The email field is checked manually so users get a clear error message instead of the database crashing when a duplicate email is submitted.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ['username', 'email', 'role', 'password1', 'password2']
model

alias of CustomUser

base_fields = {'email': <django.forms.fields.EmailField object>, 'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'role': <django.forms.fields.TypedChoiceField object>, 'username': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}
clean_email()[source]

Prevent duplicate email addresses during registration.

declared_fields = {'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}
property media

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

news.models module

class news.models.ApprovedArticleLog(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Stores records of approved articles.

This model acts as the internal logging endpoint target for approved articles, matching the task requirement to log approved article activity.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

article

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

article_id
get_next_by_posted_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: posted_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)
get_previous_by_posted_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: posted_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)
id

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>
posted_at

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

class news.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Represents a news article.

An article is written by a journalist. It may optionally belong to a publisher. If publisher is blank, the article is treated as an independent journalist article.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

approval_logs

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

approved

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

author

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

get_previous_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)

newsletters

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>
publisher

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

publisher_id
title

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

class news.models.CustomUser(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: AbstractUser

Custom user model for the news application.

Roles: - Reader: can view articles and newsletters. - Editor: can review, approve, update, and delete content. - Journalist: can create, update, and delete own content.

The user’s Django group is automatically synced to match their role.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

EDITOR = 'editor'
JOURNALIST = 'journalist'
exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

READER = 'reader'
ROLE_CHOICES = [('reader', 'Reader'), ('editor', 'Editor'), ('journalist', 'Journalist')]
articles

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

newsletters

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

password

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

role

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

save(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Save the user and keep their group/role data consistent.

A ManyToManyField cannot literally store None. The practical Django equivalent is to clear those relationships when they should not apply.

Therefore: - Readers may have subscribed publishers and subscribed journalists. - Journalists and editors have those reader-only fields cleared.

subscribed_journalists

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

subscribed_publishers

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

subscribers_to_journalist

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

sync_role_group()[source]

Put the user into the Django group matching their role.

This supports the task requirement that users should be assigned to groups based on their role.

user_permissions

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

username

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

class news.models.Newsletter(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Represents a curated collection of articles.

Newsletters are created by journalists and can contain many articles.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

articles

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

author

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

author_id
created_at

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_previous_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>
title

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

class news.models.Publisher(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Represents a publication.

A publisher can have multiple editors and multiple journalists.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

articles

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

description

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

editors

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

journalists

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

name

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>
subscribers

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

news.permissions module

class news.permissions.ArticlePermission[source]

Bases: BasePermission

API permissions for articles.

Rules: - Readers can only view approved articles. - Journalists can create articles. - Journalists can edit/delete only their own articles. - Editors can view, update, delete, and approve articles. - Superusers can do everything.

has_object_permission(request, view, obj)[source]

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

has_permission(request, view)[source]

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

class news.permissions.IsEditor[source]

Bases: BasePermission

Allows access only to editors and superusers.

Used for article approval endpoints.

has_permission(request, view)[source]

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

news.serializers module

class news.serializers.ArticleSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for articles.

The author is automatically set in the view when an article is created. The API user should not manually choose the article author.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'title', 'content', 'author', 'author_username', 'publisher', 'publisher_name', 'created_at', 'approved']
model

alias of Article

read_only_fields = ['id', 'author', 'author_username', 'publisher_name', 'created_at', 'approved']
class news.serializers.NewsletterSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for newsletters.

Articles are selected by ID when creating or updating a newsletter.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'title', 'description', 'created_at', 'author', 'author_username', 'articles', 'article_titles']
model

alias of Newsletter

read_only_fields = ['id', 'created_at', 'author', 'author_username', 'article_titles']
get_article_titles(obj)[source]

Return article titles for readable API responses.

class news.serializers.PublisherSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for publishers.

Editors and journalists are represented by their user IDs.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'name', 'description', 'editors', 'journalists']
model

alias of Publisher

class news.serializers.UserSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for custom users.

Passwords are intentionally excluded.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'username', 'email', 'role', 'subscribed_publishers', 'subscribed_journalists']
model

alias of CustomUser

read_only_fields = ['id']

news.signals module

news.signals.assign_user_to_role_group(sender, instance, **kwargs)[source]

Automatically place a user into the correct group based on their role.

news.signals.create_role_groups(sender, **kwargs)[source]

Create default user groups and assign permissions after migrations.

news.tests module

class news.tests.NewsApplicationTests(methodName='runTest')[source]

Bases: TestCase

Automated tests for the News Application capstone project.

These tests cover: - Role-based API access. - Reader subscribed article filtering. - Journalist article creation. - Editor approval and delete permissions. - Newsletter behaviour. - Approval notification/email logic.

authenticate(user)[source]

Force authenticate an API request as a specific user.

setUp()[source]

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_approval_sends_email_to_subscribed_reader()[source]
test_duplicate_email_is_not_allowed()[source]
test_editor_can_approve_article()[source]
test_editor_can_delete_article()[source]
test_journalist_can_create_article()[source]
test_newsletter_can_be_created_by_journalist()[source]
test_reader_can_only_retrieve_approved_articles()[source]
test_reader_can_retrieve_only_subscribed_content()[source]
test_reader_cannot_approve_article()[source]
test_reader_cannot_create_article()[source]
test_reader_cannot_delete_article()[source]

news.urls module

news.views module

class news.views.ArticleViewSet(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelViewSet

API endpoint for articles.

approve(request, pk=None)[source]

Allow editors to approve articles from the API.

basename = None
description = None
detail = None
get_queryset()[source]

Get the list of items for this view. This must be an iterable, and may be a queryset. Defaults to using self.queryset.

This method should always be used rather than accessing self.queryset directly, as self.queryset gets evaluated only once, and those results are cached for all subsequent requests.

You may want to override this if you need to provide different querysets depending on the incoming request.

(Eg. return a list of items that is specific to the user)

name = None
perform_create(serializer)[source]
permission_classes = [<class 'news.permissions.ArticlePermission'>]
serializer_class

alias of ArticleSerializer

subscribed(request)[source]

Return approved articles from the reader’s subscriptions.

suffix = None

suffix = None
news.views.approve_article(request, article_id)[source]

Allow editors to approve articles from the web interface.

news.views.approved_article_log_api(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]

Internal API endpoint to log approved articles.

news.views.article_create(request)[source]

Allow journalists and editors to create a new article.

news.views.article_delete(request, article_id)[source]

Allow article authors, editors, or admins to delete an article.

news.views.article_detail(request, article_id)[source]

Show one approved article.

news.views.article_list(request)[source]

Show all approved articles to readers.

news.views.article_update(request, article_id)[source]

Allow article authors, editors, or admins to update an article.

news.views.dashboard_view(request)[source]

Send each user to the correct dashboard for their role.

news.views.editor_dashboard(request)[source]

Display articles available for editor review.

news.views.editor_review(request)[source]

Allow editors to review unapproved articles.

news.views.home(request)[source]

Display the public home page.

news.views.journalist_dashboard(request)[source]

Display the journalist dashboard and their own content.

news.views.journalist_list(request)[source]

Display journalists so readers can subscribe to them.

news.views.login_view(request)[source]

Authenticate users and redirect them based on their role.

news.views.logout_view(request)[source]

Log out the current user.

news.views.newsletter_create(request)[source]

Allow journalists and editors to create newsletters.

news.views.newsletter_delete(request, newsletter_id)[source]

Allow newsletter authors, editors, or admins to delete newsletters.

news.views.newsletter_list(request)[source]

Display a list of newsletters.

news.views.newsletter_update(request, newsletter_id)[source]

Allow newsletter authors, editors, or admins to update newsletters.

news.views.notify_article_approval(article, request=None)[source]

Notify subscribed readers when an article is approved.

The task allows the approval logic to happen directly in the approval view instead of Django signals. This function supports that approach.

It checks: 1. Readers subscribed to the article publisher. 2. Readers subscribed to the article journalist/author.

It also logs the approved article in the ApprovedArticleLog model.

news.views.publisher_create(request)[source]

Allow editors and admins to create publishers.

news.views.publisher_delete(request, publisher_id)[source]

Allow editors and admins to delete publishers.

news.views.publisher_list(request)[source]

Display a list of publishers.

news.views.publisher_update(request, publisher_id)[source]

Allow editors and admins to update publishers.

news.views.reader_dashboard(request)[source]

Display the reader dashboard.

news.views.reader_newsletters(request)[source]

Display newsletters for readers.

news.views.register_view(request)[source]

Register a new user and log them in.

news.views.role_required(required_role)[source]

Restrict a view to users with a specific role.

Superusers are allowed through so the project can still be tested easily from the admin account.

news.views.subscribe_journalist(request, journalist_id)[source]

Allow readers to subscribe to a journalist.

news.views.subscribe_publisher(request, publisher_id)[source]

Allow readers to subscribe to a publisher.

Module contents
